title: 02 tina notices the blooming rule
prompt: Use case: identity-preserve. Asset type: cinematic storyboard frame. Image 1 is the foreground, identity, product, car, and camera authority. Image 2 supplies the architectural bloom language. Continue the same moment. Preserve Tina, wardrobe, seat, candy, Peelerz bag, and rideshare interior, but let Tina turn her eyes and head toward the passenger window in quiet recognition. Outside, the nearest house is still closed, the next house is midway through a beautiful physical bloom, and a farther house is fully open. Roof planes lift as structural petals, walls hinge apart without breaking, and warm inhabited rooms unfold around a luminous interior garden. The effect advances toward the moving car from far to near. The car remains intact, but one delicate curved seam has appeared in the metal above the window, echoing the seams in the houses. No chaos and no destruction; make the rule newly legible.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one frame from a coherent live-action Peelerz commercial sequence. Treat every input image according to its explicitly stated role. Maintain Tina as the same young woman with the same face, long brown hair, blue jacket, black top, and quiet curious presence. Maintain the pink lychee gummy and pink Peelerz package. The impossible event is elegant, physical, and benign. It follows one discoverable rule rather than becoming generalized fantasy. Photoreal cinematic advertising, tactile practical-effects credibility, no captions, no panel borders, no watermark, no extra characters, no random rainbow candyland.
